heading: Agreements with Federated States of Micronesia
label: 1984 Agreements with Federated States of Micronesia
notes: References in TextSection 209(j), referred to in subsec. (a)(2), means .section 209(j) of title II of div. G of Pub. L. 118–42

children.label: A
children.text: to develop and adequately enforce laws of the Federated States of Micronesia; and

children.label: B
children.text: to cooperate with the United States in the enforcement of criminal laws of the United States.

children.heading: Use of appropriated funds
children.label: 2 Use of appropriated funds
children.text: Funds appropriated pursuant to subsection (j) of (as amended by section 209(j)) may be used in accordance with .section 1921d of this titlesection 1921a(a) of this title

children.heading: United States appointees to Joint Economic Management Committee
children.label: b United States appointees to Joint Economic Management Committee
A
children.text: be voting members of the Committee; and

children.label: B
children.text: continue to be officers or employees of the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: B
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Interior, in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: C
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Interagency Group on Freely Associated States established under .section 1987(d)(1) of this title

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: 3 Reappointment
children.text: A United States member of the Committee appointed under paragraph (2) may be reappointed for not more than 2 additional 2-year terms.

children.label: A
children.text: by reason of knowledge, experience, or training, are especially qualified in accounting, auditing, budget analysis, compliance, grant administration, program management, or international economics; and

children.label: B
children.text: possess not less than 5 years of full-time experience in accounting, auditing, budget analysis, compliance, grant administration, program management, or international economics.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of appointment of a United States member of the Committee under paragraph (2), the Secretary of the Interior shall notify the appropriate committees of Congress that an individual has been appointed as a voting member of the Committee under that paragraph, including a statement prepared by the Secretary of the Interior attesting to the qualifications of the member described in paragraph (4), subject to subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a member appointed under paragraph (2)(A), the Secretary of the Interior shall compile information on the member provided to the Secretary of the Interior by the Secretary of State on request of the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a member appointed under paragraph (2)(C), the Secretary of the Interior shall compile information on the member provided to the Secretary of the Interior by the Interagency Group on Freely Associated States established under on request of the Secretary of the Interior.section 1987(d)(1) of this title

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: 6 Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the Committee receives or completes any report required under the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact, or any related subsidiary agreement, the Secretary of the Interior shall submit the report to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: if the report is submitted by the applicable deadline, written notice attesting that the report is complete and accurate; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the report is not submitted by the applicable deadline, written notice that the report has not been timely submitted.

children.heading: United States appointees to Joint Trust Fund Committee
children.label: c United States appointees to Joint Trust Fund Committee

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The 3 United States voting members (which are composed of the United States chair and 2 other members from the Government of the United States) to the Joint Trust Fund Committee established pursuant to the agreement described in section 462(b)(5) of the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact (referred to in this subsection as the “Committee”) shall continue to be officers or employees of the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Secretary of State.

children.label: B
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: C
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: 3 Reappointment
children.text: A United States member of the Committee appointed under paragraph (2) may be reappointed for not more than 2 additional 2-year terms.

children.label: A
children.text: by reason of knowledge, experience, or training, are especially qualified in accounting, auditing, budget analysis, compliance, financial investment, grant administration, program management, or international economics; and

children.label: B
children.text: possess not less than 5 years of full-time experience in accounting, auditing, budget analysis, compliance, financial investment, grant administration, program management, or international economics.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of appointment of a United States member to the Committee under paragraph (2), the Secretary of the Interior shall notify the appropriate committees of Congress that an individual has been appointed as a voting member of the Committee under that paragraph, including a statement attesting to the qualifications of the member described in paragraph (4), subject to subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a member appointed under paragraph (2)(A), the Secretary of the Interior shall compile information on the member provided to the Secretary of the Interior by the Secretary of State on request of the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a member appointed under paragraph (2)(C), the Secretary of the Interior shall compile information on the member provided to the Secretary of the Interior by the Secretary of the Treasury on request of the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: 6 Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the Committee receives or completes any report required under the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact, or any related subsidiary agreement, the Secretary of the Interior shall submit the report to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: if the report is submitted by the applicable deadline, written notice attesting that the report is complete and accurate; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the report is not submitted by the applicable deadline, written notice that the report has not been timely submitted.
